# nerdctl_container (Data Source)

An existing container, read via `nerdctl container inspect`. Fails when absent.

## Example Usage

```terraform
data "nerdctl_container" "app" {
  name = "app"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Container name.

### Read-Only

- `cpus` (Number) CPU limit in cores derived from the cgroup quota and period, or `0` when unlimited.
- `env` (Map of String) Every environment variable in the container's spec, including image and runtime defaults.
- `id` (String) Container ID.
- `image` (String) Image the container runs, with the implied `docker.io` registry prefixes stripped.
- `labels` (Map of String) Labels on the container (nerdctl and containerd bookkeeping labels are filtered out).
- `memory` (Number) Memory limit in bytes, or `0` when unlimited.
- `networks` (List of String) Networks the container is attached to, in interface order.
- `pid` (Number) Host PID of the container's main process, or `0` when not running.
- `ports` (Attributes List) Published ports. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--ports))
- `privileged` (Boolean) Whether the container runs with extended privileges.
- `restart` (String) Restart policy: `no`, `always`, `unless-stopped`, or `on-failure[:max-retries]`.
- `running` (Boolean) Whether the container is currently running.
- `status` (String) Container state: `created`, `running`, `paused`, `restarting`, `removing`, `exited`, or `dead`.

<a id="nestedatt--ports"></a>
### Nested Schema for `ports`

Read-Only:

- `external` (Number) Port published on the host.
- `internal` (Number) Port inside the container.
- `protocol` (String) `tcp`, `udp`, or `sctp`.
